function:
def priority(item: float, bins: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Returns priority with which we want to add item to each bin.

    Args:
        item: Size of item to be added to the bin.
        bins: Array of capacities for each bin.

    Return:
        Array of same size as bins with priority score of each bin.
    """
    remaining_capacity = bins - item
    variance_reduction = np.var(bins) - np.var(remaining_capacity)
    priorities = -(remaining_capacity) + variance_reduction
    return priorities
thought: To improve the bin-packing heuristic, we can prioritize bins that not only have sufficient remaining capacity but also minimize the leftover space after placing the item, while also considering the distribution of remaining capacities among bins to avoid overfilling any single bin. This can be achieved by combining two key signals: the negative of the remaining capacity after placing the item (`-(bins - item)`) to favor bins with less leftover space, and the variance of the remaining capacities of all bins (`np.var(bins)`) to encourage even distribution, resulting in a higher score for bins that reduce variance when the item is placed.
